commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=31244ff7d0cebd15d4b2300ab54aa34422b6a72c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds context-window cache warmup at gateway/status paths, switches CLI status runtime resolution to the shared agent resolver, and adds context lookup regression tests.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests +129. Total +139 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: the CLI status summary still passes allowAsyncLoad:false into the resolver, while the PR's new warmup branch only fires when that flag is not false. I did not run a live command because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs author or maintainer updates for the status-summary warmup/config-scope defects plus real behavior proof; ClawSweeper repair should not run while the external proof gate is missing.

target.title: fix(status): warm model context-window cache before /status reads it
target.body:
## Problem

\/status\ shows \200K\ (DEFAULT_CONTEXT_TOKENS) for any model discovered from the provider catalog, regardless of the model's actual context window. For example, \^Gnthropic/claude-opus-4.7-20260219\ (1M) would display as \200K\.

## Root Cause

Three independent gaps conspired to keep MODEL_CONTEXT_TOKEN_CACHE empty when the \/status\ command read it:

1. **No startup warming**: \server-startup-early.ts\ fired \oid primeRemoteSkillsCache()\ but had no equivalent call for context-window cache
2. **prepareContextWindowCache short-circuit**: When callers pass a config struct (\cfg\), \skipRuntimeConfigLoad=true\ caused the function to return immediately — skipping even async cache warming
3. **Import bypass**: \status.summary.runtime.ts\ imported \esolveContextTokensForModelFromCache\ directly from \context-resolution.js\, bypassing the warming wrapper in \context.ts\
4. **Status explicitly disabled warming**: \status-text.ts\ passed \^GllowAsyncLoad: false\, preventing the status path from triggering cache warming

## Fix (4 files, +8/-2 lines)

| File | Change | Gap Closed |
|------|--------|-----------|
| \src/gateway/server-startup-early.ts\ | Add \oid ensureContextWindowCacheLoaded()\ at startup (alongside existing \primeRemoteSkillsCache()\) | #1 — startup warming |
| \src/agents/context.ts\ | When \skipRuntimeConfigLoad=true\ and \^GllowAsyncLoad !== false\, still fire \oid ensureContextWindowCacheLoaded()\ | #2 — short-circuit |
| \src/status/status-text.ts\ | Remove \^GllowAsyncLoad: false\ from \esolveStatusRuntimeContextTokens\ | #4 — status disabled warming |
| \src/commands/status.summary.runtime.ts\ | Import \esolveContextTokensForModel\ from \context.js\ (warming wrapper) instead of \context-resolution.js\ (raw cache) | #3 — import bypass |

## Testing

- 4 regression tests added in \context.lookup.test.ts\:
  - **Critical**: warming fires when \skipRuntimeConfigLoad=true\ and \^GllowAsyncLoad\ is unspecified
  - **Critical**: warming does NOT fire when \^GllowAsyncLoad=false\ (preserves read-only contract)
  - **High**: cold cache → second call returns discovered tokens
  - **High**: startup warming → status call returns discovered tokens
- All existing tests pass with no modifications needed
- Existing \context.test.ts\ tests always pass \^GllowAsyncLoad: false\ explicitly → zero behavioral change

## Verification Trace

Before:
\\\
Startup ───→ (no cache warming)
Status command → resolveStatusRuntimeContextTokens
  → resolveContextTokensForModel({ cfg, ..., allowAsyncLoad: false })
    → prepareContextWindowCache(skip=true, allowAsyncLoad=false)
      → return immediately (NO warming)
    → resolveContextTokensForModelFromCache → empty → 200K← BUG
\\\

After:
\\\
Startup ──→ void ensureContextWindowCacheLoaded()
  → primeConfiguredContextWindows()
  → async: load model catalog → MODEL_CONTEXT_TOKEN_CACHE populated← STARTUP FIX

Status command → resolveStatusRuntimeContextTokens
  → resolveContextTokensForModel({ cfg, ..., allowAsyncLoad: undefined })
    → prepareContextWindowCache(skip=true, allowAsyncLoad=undefined)
      → void ensureContextWindowCacheLoaded() (fires!—singleton guarded)
    → resolveContextTokensForModelFromCache → populated → correct value← FIXED
\\\

## Design Decisions

- **Fire-and-forget pattern**: Uses \oid\ (matching existing \primeRemoteSkillsCache()\). First status call may still see cold cache if warming hasn't completed — acceptable because (a) startup fires warming early, (b) 200K is a conservative fallback, (c) every subsequent call will be correct
- **Backward-compatible**: \^GllowAsyncLoad !== false\ correctly distinguishes \undefined\ (default → fire warming) from \ alse\ (explicitly denied → skip warming)
- **No slash-model behavior change**: Unlike the previous comprehensive attempt (PR #92772), this fix does not touch \status-message.ts\ or model-ID parsing — only cache warming

## Related

- Closes #92760: CLI openclaw status shows 200K context window
- Related PR #92772 (superseded — narrower scope)
